title: Halcyon's Green Finance Exposure Minor, Analysts Say, Despite Northveil Fallout
body:
Investors worried about fallout from the Northveil Energy Partners green bond collapse got some reassurance Friday: Halcyon Genomics' exposure to the failed offering was minimal, according to two sell-side analysts who cover the biotech.

Halcyon had been named in a report this week as a prospective buyer for the Northveil bond, which imploded after an ESG ratings downgrade. But analysts at Linden Capital Markets and Orion Healthcare Equity both said Halcyon's treasury policies limit green bond exposure to a small slice of its overall cash portfolio.

"This is noise," wrote Orion analyst Kevin Fitch in a note Thursday. "Halcyon's real story right now is Oryza integration and whether Ferrara's rare disease bet pays off."

Halcyon CEO Dominic Ferrara did not address the Northveil situation in any public forum this week. The company's spokesperson said only that Halcyon "does not comment on individual treasury positions."

Shares of Halcyon recovered slightly this week after falling nearly seven percent following the Oryza acquisition announcement. The stock closed Friday at $71.40.

Dr. Lena Vasquez, who founded Oryza and is now heading Halcyon's rare disease division, gave her first public remarks since the deal closed at a conference in Boston Thursday, focusing entirely on the science of her CRISPR programs.
